skill.name: appflowy-api
skill.description: Automates AppFlowy Cloud and GoTrue API tasks for self-hosted AppFlowy deployments, including authentication, workspace discovery, document updates, database rows, search, and collab repair workflows.
skill.summary: Run AppFlowy API authentication, workspace, document, database, search, and collab automation from guided scripts.
skill.author: BaloneGit

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, environment, filesystem, and heuristic hits. Review found no prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent, but the skill legitimately handles credentials, makes authenticated AppFlowy requests, and can mutate or delete workspace content. Publish with clear warnings for trusted self-hosted environments and careful credential handling.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated AppFlowy Network Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill sends credentials to a configured GoTrue endpoint and sends bearer-token requests to AppFlowy workspace, document, database, and row endpoints. This is expected for the skill, but a wrong base URL or exposed token could affect private workspace data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTTP client and login flow are explicit and semantically match the advertised AppFlowy automation purpose. The risk depends on user-supplied endpoints and credentials, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Workspace Content Mutation and Deletion
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill can create page views, append blocks, upsert rows, repair collab state, and delete document blocks or row orders. These operations are legitimate but can cause data loss if run against the wrong workspace or with an unsafe template.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly performs create, update, and delete operations on AppFlowy content after authentication. The behavior is documented and not malicious, but the potential impact is real.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Local Payload Handling
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The scripts can read optional .env files, config files, and JSON payload files, then use credentials or tokens for API calls. This is normal for API tooling, but users must avoid untrusted files and protect token output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file and environment access is explicit and opt-in in several places, and token retrieval is the stated purpose. The remaining concern is accidental disclosure or misuse of sensitive local inputs.

low_findings.title: Constrained Subprocess Use
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for command injection. subprocess.run invokes Python or Node with local script paths selected from fixed command maps, and user arguments are passed as argument arrays. This still requires Node and local dependency trust.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command targets are fixed local scripts and no shell string is constructed. I did not find evidence of user-controlled shell command injection in the reviewed locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Private Example Endpoint
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE but low severity. The documentation and sample config include a private IP address for AppFlowy and GoTrue examples. It is not a public exfiltration endpoint, but users should replace it before running commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is visible in examples and uses a private address. The risk is misconfiguration rather than malicious outbound traffic.

low_findings.title: Static Heuristic False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Weak-crypto, obfuscation, and reconnaissance hits appear to come from descriptions, metadata, endpoint references, non-English text, and normal API troubleshooting text. No prompt injection attempt or encoded payload was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain metadata, documentation, and endpoint examples rather than cryptographic code or obfuscated execution. This finding is based on inspected context, not just pattern matching.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Requests With Bearer Tokens
dangerous_patterns.description: The shared client builds HTTP requests and attaches Authorization headers when a token is provided. This is necessary for AppFlowy automation, but tokens can reach any configured base URL.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The header construction and request dispatch are direct and clear. The security impact depends on the configured AppFlowy URL.

dangerous_patterns.title: Opt-In Environment File Reading
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can read a user-specified .env file and environment variables for AppFlowy URLs, version, and device ID. The README states this is opt-in, reducing but not removing secret-handling risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code paths for environment resolution are explicit. I did not find automatic .env loading without a supplied path in these locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Execution for Collab Updates
dangerous_patterns.description: Python code starts fixed Node helper scripts to transform Yjs collab state. This is not shell injection, but it executes local JavaScript and requires trusted dependencies.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess and dependency use are visible and constrained to known files. The risk is supply-chain and local execution trust, not arbitrary shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Collab State Editing
dangerous_patterns.description: Node helpers delete document blocks, text maps, and row order entries from Yjs state. This can intentionally clean templates, but it can remove real content when input IDs are wrong.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Deletion behavior is direct and semantically confirmed by function names and code. It is legitimate maintenance behavior with meaningful data-loss risk.

content.value_statement: Self-hosted AppFlowy automation often requires repeatable authentication, workspace lookup, and database updates. This skill provides guided scripts and references for those API workflows.
actual_capabilities: Fetch GoTrue access tokens with email and password credentials.
List workspaces and databases from a configured AppFlowy deployment.
Search workspace content through the AppFlowy search endpoint.
Create page views, append document blocks, and update page names.
Apply Grid templates, create fields, upsert rows, and repair select options.
Run doctor checks for GoTrue, AppFlowy health, workspace access, and version mismatch warnings.
limitations: Targets AppFlowy Cloud 0.12.3 behavior and may need changes for other versions.
Requires valid AppFlowy URLs, credentials, tokens, workspace IDs, and view IDs.
Can modify or delete workspace content when templates or IDs are wrong.
Uses local Python and Node scripts, including the yjs dependency for collab updates.

use_cases.title: Validate a self-hosted deployment
use_cases.description: Run health, authentication, workspace, and version checks before using automation in a live AppFlowy workspace.

use_cases.title: Build repeatable workspace setup scripts
use_cases.description: Create documents, add Grid sections, define fields, and populate rows from reusable templates.

use_cases.title: Debug AppFlowy API behavior
use_cases.description: Inspect endpoint requirements, headers, token flow, search responses, and database row details during integration work.

prompt_templates.title: Check deployment health
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AppFlowy API skill to run a doctor check for my configured AppFlowy and GoTrue URLs. Explain any failed checks.

prompt_templates.title: List available workspaces
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my AppFlowy token or email login to list workspaces. Show the workspace IDs I need for later commands.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Grid template
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the provided Grid template to this workspace and view. Tell me what fields, rows, and relation updates will be created.

prompt_templates.title: Repair collab data safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AppFlowy document and prepare a safe collab cleanup plan. Use clean-only first and identify any destructive operations.

output_examples.input: Run a doctor check against my self-hosted AppFlowy deployment.
output_examples.output: A concise health report with GoTrue status, AppFlowy health status, workspace access status, and version compatibility warnings.

output_examples.input: Apply the fitness plan Grid template to a document view.
output_examples.output: A summary of the Grid database created or reused, default rows removed, fields added, rows inserted, and relation cells updated.

output_examples.input: Search a workspace for a phrase.
output_examples.output: A readable list of matching AppFlowy results with IDs and context suitable for follow-up API calls.

best_practices: Use a test workspace before running create, update, delete, or collab repair commands.
Pass tokens and .env files only from trusted local paths and avoid logging sensitive output.
Run clean-only or doctor checks before applying templates to production documents.
anti_patterns: Do not use the sample private IP address without replacing it with your own deployment URL.
Do not run templates from untrusted sources against a production workspace.
Do not share command output that contains access tokens, workspace IDs, or private document data.

faq.question: Does this skill work with AppFlowy Cloud SaaS?
faq.answer: It is written for self-hosted AppFlowy Cloud and GoTrue deployments. Other environments may require endpoint or version changes.

faq.question: Does it read .env files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The documentation and code use .env files only when an explicit --env path is provided.

faq.question: Can this skill change existing AppFlowy content?
faq.answer: Yes. Several scripts create pages, append blocks, update rows, repair collab data, and remove default or invalid rows.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: You need an access token or an email and password that can obtain a GoTrue token for the target deployment.

faq.question: Why does it need Node.js?
faq.answer: Some collab repair workflows use local Node scripts with the yjs dependency to transform AppFlowy collaborative document state.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It has medium risk. It is publishable with warnings because it handles credentials and can modify workspace data.
